verses.text: 'Learn not the way of the heathen'; their idols are cut from a tree, nailed fast, decked with silver, 'upright as the palm tree, but speak not.' They cannot do good or evil. Lifeless idols are nothing to fear and nothing to trust.

'Learn not the way of the heathen'; their idols are cut from a tree, nailed fast, decked with silver, 'upright as the palm tree, but speak not.' They cannot do good or evil. Lifeless idols are nothing to fear and nothing to trust.

'Learn not the way of the heathen'; their idols are cut from a tree, nailed fast, decked with silver, 'upright as the palm tree, but speak not.' They cannot do good or evil. Lifeless idols are nothing to fear and nothing to trust.

'Learn not the way of the heathen'; their idols are cut from a tree, nailed fast, decked with silver, 'upright as the palm tree, but speak not.' They cannot do good or evil. Lifeless idols are nothing to fear and nothing to trust.

'Learn not the way of the heathen'; their idols are cut from a tree, nailed fast, decked with silver, 'upright as the palm tree, but speak not.' They cannot do good or evil. Lifeless idols are nothing to fear and nothing to trust.
'There is none like unto thee, O LORD'; who would not fear the King of nations? His greatness is unmatched. The living God towers infinitely above every idol of the heathen.
'There is none like unto thee, O LORD'; who would not fear the King of nations? His greatness is unmatched. The living God towers infinitely above every idol of the heathen.
The idol-worshippers are 'brutish and foolish,' their gods mere silver and craftwork from afar. Their religion is empty. Skilled labour cannot make a god out of metal and wood.
The idol-worshippers are 'brutish and foolish,' their gods mere silver and craftwork from afar. Their religion is empty. Skilled labour cannot make a god out of metal and wood.
'The LORD is the true God... the living God, and an everlasting king'; the gods that made not the heavens 'shall perish.' He alone is real. The false gods will vanish, but the LORD endures for ever.
'The LORD is the true God... the living God, and an everlasting king'; the gods that made not the heavens 'shall perish.' He alone is real. The false gods will vanish, but the LORD endures for ever.
He made the earth by His power and stretched out the heavens by His wisdom; at His voice the waters multiply. Creation is His work. The God who formed the world is no rival to lifeless idols.
He made the earth by His power and stretched out the heavens by His wisdom; at His voice the waters multiply. Creation is His work. The God who formed the world is no rival to lifeless idols.

Every idol-maker is brutish, his images a lie; 'the portion of Jacob is not like them: for he is the former of all things.' God is His people's portion. To have the Maker of all as our God is wealth beyond idols.

Every idol-maker is brutish, his images a lie; 'the portion of Jacob is not like them: for he is the former of all things.' God is His people's portion. To have the Maker of all as our God is wealth beyond idols.

Every idol-maker is brutish, his images a lie; 'the portion of Jacob is not like them: for he is the former of all things.' God is His people's portion. To have the Maker of all as our God is wealth beyond idols.
God bids the besieged gather their goods, for He will 'sling out the inhabitants of the land at this once.' Exile is near. The judgment long warned of now comes swiftly upon them.
God bids the besieged gather their goods, for He will 'sling out the inhabitants of the land at this once.' Exile is near. The judgment long warned of now comes swiftly upon them.

Jeremiah laments his hurt, his tent spoiled, his children gone, for 'the pastors are become brutish' and scattered the flock. Bad shepherds ruined the people. When leaders forsake God, the whole flock is scattered.

Jeremiah laments his hurt, his tent spoiled, his children gone, for 'the pastors are become brutish' and scattered the flock. Bad shepherds ruined the people. When leaders forsake God, the whole flock is scattered.

Jeremiah laments his hurt, his tent spoiled, his children gone, for 'the pastors are become brutish' and scattered the flock. Bad shepherds ruined the people. When leaders forsake God, the whole flock is scattered.

Hearing the invader's noise, Jeremiah prays, 'O LORD, correct me, but with judgment; not in thine anger,' and asks God to pour His fury on the heathen who devour Jacob. He submits to chastening. The believer accepts correction but pleads for mercy in measure.
